title: Reduced Guns Office DOJ Grant Reprogramming -- Unbelievable and OG's Out, Funds to Southwest Center and Good Life
summary: Latif Johnson-Kenzie (Director, Reduced Guns Office) presents fund reprogramming across items 33-36. Item 33: Rescind Unbelievable contract ($186,472) -- signed 2022, no work performed in two years. Chief Strategy Officer Stephanie Pasqual clarifies contract also expired Jan 31, 2026. Item 35: OG's contract expired; both organizations' funds redistributed to Southwest Center (Syracuse Model Neighborhood, $272,278) and Good Life (with Salvation Army for victim family services). Grant expires Sept 30, 2026 -- Biden-era DOJ, no similar grants under current administration. EXALT program (item 37, held for budget correction) co-located with On Point on Harrison Street, started ~3 weeks ago with cohort of 10 youth from probation/Good Life referrals. Council demands committee meeting with agency presentations, org chart, and full DOJ budget before voting.

political_signal.description: Council demands committee meeting, org chart, budget transparency, and agency capacity assessment before approving DOJ grant fund redistribution

processing_notes: Reduced Guns Office is under the Mayor's Office. DOJ grant is one-time Biden-era award expiring Sept 30, 2026. Unbelievable had contract since 2022 with no activity. OG's had separate Safer Streets contract rescinded previously -- this is a different DOJ contract. EXALT co-located with On Point and EOC on Harrison Street. Syracuse 360 mentioned as separate DOJ application. Coalition meetings: 1st Thursdays 2:30pm, 2nd Thursdays 11am faith leaders, 3rd Thursdays 10am CBOs at Bernett.
Segment 13 of 23 from Common Council Study Session, Wednesday March 18th 2026
Transcript corrections applied: CNS -> CNS Engineers; SU -> Syracuse University
Potential duplicate detected: overlaps with CTX-COUNCIL-2026-00065 -- linked, not skipped
